text: every cooking show acts like we all have 47 tiny bowls pre-measured and ready to go. i dont even own 47 bowls
recipe blogs be like 'this reminds me of summers in tuscany' maam i just need to know how long to boil an egg
my air fryer is my therapist now. she listens, she heats, she never judges
instant pot changed my life and i will not be taking questions at this time
meal prepped 5 days of chicken and rice. ate it once. ordered doordash the other 4 days
grocery budget: $80/week. actual spend: $80 at trader joes on snacks alone lmao
i have spices from 2019 and honestly they might be older than some of my friendships
my husband says leftovers are fine for 5 days and i say thats attempted murder
restaurant gave me 3 ravioli for $24. i could cry. i did cry actually
tip calculator anxiety is real. i just round up to whatever number makes me look generous
gordon ramsay yells at people for raw chicken but my mom been serving that since 92
the recipe said 30 minutes. the recipe lied. its been 2 hours and my smoke alarm agrees
air fryer facebook groups are literally a religion and im fully converted
put everything in the instant pot. potatoes, sadness, a whole chicken. dinner in 40 min
meal prep sunday is just anxiety with tupperware
spent $200 on groceries and theres nothing to eat. explain that to me. please
my spice rack is 90% garlic powder and paprika and i refuse to be shamed for it
leftover pizza is better cold and i will die on this hill
the portions at cheesecake factory could feed a village tho so no complaints there
i tip 20% minimum bc i worked food service and those people deserve hazard pay
cooking shows: 'just add a splash of wine' me: adds a splash to the pan and a glass to myself
why does every recipe blog need a 3000 word essay before the actual recipe. im starving
air fryer tater tots changed me as a person. im not who i was before
my instant pot has a saute function and thats basically witchcraft
i meal prep for the version of me that doesnt exist. the one with discipline
bought organic everything once. my wallet still hasnt forgiven me
found cumin from 2017 in my cabinet. does cumin expire? dont answer that
my roommate eats my leftovers and calls it 'sharing'. i call it theft
got a kids portion at a restaurant and it was bigger than the adult one. make it make sense
calculating tip after splitting the bill 6 ways is my personal hell
cooking shows never show the part where you wash 37 dishes afterwards
the jump to recipe button is the greatest invention of the 21st century
air fryer salmon at 2am hits different. dont ask me why im awake
instant pot rice is either perfection or cement. there is no in between
i buy meal prep containers like buying them means ill actually use them
aldi trip for 5 things. spent $93. how does this keep happening
my spice drawer is a graveyard of good intentions
eating leftovers on day 4 is a trust exercise with yourself
applebees portions used to be huge now they serving appetizer sizes for entree prices
i just venmo whatever the tip calculator says bc i cannot do math after 2 drinks
every cooking competition: 'i put my heart and soul into this dish' bro its a risotto
recipe: prep time 10 min. reality: 10 min just finding where i put the garlic press
the air fryer community on reddit is unhinged and i am here for every post
instant pot yogurt people are on another level of commitment and i respect it
meal prepped beautiful lunches. forgot them at home every single day this week
grocery store rotisserie chicken for $5.99 is carrying my entire household
i alphabetized my spices once in 2020 and have been coasting on that energy since
3 day old chinese food is a delicacy and i wont hear otherwise
olive garden unlimited breadsticks is the only honest restaurant deal left
tipping on takeout feels weird but not tipping feels worse so here we are
nigella lawson making a midnight snack like its a spiritual experience meanwhile im eating shredded cheese at 1am
food bloggers act like making banana bread is a personality trait
air fried a whole head of broccoli last night. transcendent. no notes
my instant pot sealing ring smells like every meal ive ever made simultaneously
i prep 5 salads on sunday. by wednesday im googling nearest taco bell
eggs were $2 now theyre $7 and im reconsidering my entire breakfast identity
got like 14 half empty bottles of hot sauce. cant throw em out. its a collection now
my partner says soup is not a meal. we may not survive this disagreement
ordered a salad for $18 and it was literally 4 leaves of lettuce. robbery
tipped 25% bc the waiter was cute and now i cant afford gas this week
chopped makes cooking look like an olympic sport when really its just panic
recipe: 'season to taste' ok but i have no taste thats why im reading a recipe
put a frozen burrito in the air fryer and it came out restaurant quality. im shaking
instant pot black beans from dry in 45 min. the future is now
i plan meals for the week then get home tired and make cereal. every time
costco bulk buying is a scam bc now i have 5 lbs of quinoa i'll never finish
there's a spice called asafoetida in my cabinet. i dont remember buying it. i dont know what it does
leftover thanksgiving turkey on day 5 is a war crime and grandma knows it
went to a fancy place. $40 steak was the size of a playing card. had mcdonalds after
the tablet at every restaurant asking for a tip before i even sit down needs to stop
barefoot contessa saying 'store bought is fine' is the most comforting thing on tv
food blogs putting life stories before recipes should be illegal. actually illegal
air fryer chicken wings > deep fried and i dont care who i offend
my instant pot has been on my counter for 3 years. used it twice. its decorative now
prepped a weeks worth of overnight oats. ate them all by tuesday. no regrets
inflation turned my grocery list into a wishlist
found bay leaves from the obama administration in my spice drawer. still using em
someone told me leftovers are just 'planned overs' and i've never been more annoyed
went to a brunch spot. $16 for 2 eggs and toast. i can make that for 80 cents
just realized ive been tipping on the total after tax this whole time. oh well
masterchef judges crying over a dessert while im crying over my burnt grilled cheese
every recipe: 'first make a roux' me: what is a roux and why does it hate me
my air fryer does more work than anyone in this house including me
instant pot cheesecake is real and its spectacular. fight me
i freeze meal prep and then forget about it. my freezer is a food museum
tried to coupon at whole foods. the cashier laughed. fair enough
my everything bagel seasoning is running out and im having a genuine crisis
eating week old pasta bc im built different (broke)
restaurant mac and cheese for $15 vs kraft for $1. kraft wins. sorry not sorry
do i tip the self checkout machine too at this point? nothing surprises me anymore
rachel ray said 30 minute meals but she clearly has elves doing her prep
a recipe told me to 'fold in the cheese' and i had a schitts creek moment
air fryer donuts at 6am on a saturday. this is what peak living looks like
the instant pot facebook group has 3 million members and they are all unwell (affectionate)
meal prep is just eating the same disappointing thing 5 times in a row
spent $12 on a single bell pepper. locally sourced from where? narnia?
i keep buying turmeric forgetting i already have 4 jars. the golden curse
my coworker microwaves fish in the office. leftovers should have rules
got 6 shrimp in my shrimp scampi for $28. counted em twice hoping id miscounted
my friend calculates the tip to the exact penny and honestly its exhausting to watch
